title: Correlation vs. Causation Challenge
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive quiz-style MicroSim where students classify relationships between correlated environmental variables as causal, reverse causal, confounded, or truly causal.

educational.topic: Correlation vs. Causation in Environmental Data
learningObjectives: Distinguish between correlation and causation in environmental datasets
Identify confounding variables that may explain observed correlations
Evaluate the strength of evidence supporting causal claims
Apply critical thinking skills to environmental data interpretation
prerequisites: Basic understanding of scatter plots and trend lines
Familiarity with variables in scientific studies

keyQuestions: Does this scatter plot show that one variable causes the other?
What confounding variables could explain this correlation?
What evidence would you need to establish true causation?
How might this correlation be misused in environmental policy arguments?
commonMisconceptions: If two variables are correlated, one must cause the other
A strong correlation is sufficient evidence for causation
If A happens before B, then A must cause B
assessmentOpportunities: Score tracking across 8-10 scenarios measures ability to classify relationships
Written explanations for each scenario reveal depth of reasoning
Extension activity applying skills to real news headlines
